original_hypothesis.title: Penalty Fee Denomination Mismatch With 6-Decimal USDT Makes Early Delisting Impossible
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: penaltyFee is initialized to `10 ether` (10 * 10^18) in MarketplaceSetting's constructor. If the configured USDT token has 6 decimals (standard on Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum), this represents 10 trillion USDT. The deployment script (DeploySecondSwap.s.sol) does not call setPenaltyFee after deployment. Early delisting becomes impossible for all sellers until admin manually fixes the fee.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The constructor sets `penaltyFee = 10 ether`. USDT on target chains (Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum) has 6 decimals. 10 * 10^18 / 10^6 = 10^13 USDT = 10 trillion USDT. No user can afford this penalty fee.
deployment_assumptions: Admin does not call setPenaltyFee after deployment
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: SecondSwap_MarketplaceSetting.sol constructor line: penaltyFee = 10 ether; DeploySecondSwap.s.sol does not call setPenaltyFee post-deployment; Target chains use 6-decimal USDT

proof_of_signal.pattern: penaltyFee = 10 ether — uses 18-decimal denomination for a 6-decimal token

preconditions: Admin has not called setPenaltyFee to adjust the value post-deployment

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Hardcoded penalty fee uses ether denomination (10^18) mismatched with USDT decimals (10^6)
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: 10 * 10^18 USDT with 6 decimals = 10 trillion USDT penalty
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No deployment script step to calibrate penalty fee for token decimals

analysis_questions: Does the deployment script ever call setPenaltyFee?
What is the default USDT token on target chains?
How long could this DoS persist before admin notice?
validation_requirements: Check deployment script for setPenaltyFee call
Verify USDT decimals on target chains
Test unlistVesting with the default penalty fee

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Protocol deploys to Base with 6-decimal USDT. Default penaltyFee = 10e18 USDT. Any seller who lists and wants to unlist before minListingDuration passes must pay 10 trillion USDT, which is impossible. Sellers' tokens are locked until minListingDuration passes or admin fixes the fee.
prerequisites: Default configuration deployed without admin intervention on penalty fee

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: All early unlisting blocked; seller tokens locked for minListingDuration
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All marketplace sellers during the misconfigured period
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Admin can fix via setPenaltyFee; no permanent fund loss

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test validating penalty fee against actual USDT decimals

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Immediately detectable when first seller tries to unlist early

validation_result.impact_summary: Default penaltyFee of 10e18 with 6-decimal USDT equals 10 trillion USDT, making early unlisting impossible until admin manually calls setPenaltyFee.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The SecondSwap_MarketplaceSetting constructor sets `penaltyFee = 10 ether` which equals 10 * 10^18 in raw units. This penalty is collected in USDT via `safeTransferFrom`. Standard USDT on Ethereum, Base, and Arbitrum has 6 decimals, so 10 * 10^18 smallest USDT units = 10^13 USDT = 10 trillion USDT. No user can afford this. The deployment script (DeploySecondSwap.s.sol) creates MarketplaceSetting with the constructor defaults and never calls setPenaltyFee afterward. The NatSpec comment even says 'Fee charged for early unlisting (in wei)' confirming the developer thought in 18-decimal terms while the actual token has 6 decimals. This is a clear denomination mismatch bug in the default configuration.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The constructor in SecondSwap_MarketplaceSetting.sol explicitly sets `penaltyFee = 10 ether`. The unlistVesting function in SecondSwap_Marketplace.sol requires the user's USDT balance >= penaltyFee and transfers penaltyFee amount of USDT. DeploySecondSwap.s.sol creates the MarketplaceSetting but never adjusts penaltyFee post-deployment. The code path is clear and the defect is evident.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: penaltyFee = 10 ether sets the value to 10 * 10^18. Since the penalty is collected in USDT (6 decimals), this represents 10 trillion USDT - an impossibly large amount that prevents any early unlisting.

The penalty fee amount (10e18 raw USDT units = 10 trillion USDT) is required and transferred. The balance check will always fail for any real user, causing the require to revert and blocking early unlisting.

calling_sequence: 2. Listing status check: listing.status == Status.LIST passes
3. Seller authorization check: listing.seller == msg.sender passes
4. msg.sender != s2Admin, so enters seller penalty block
5. listing.listTime + minListingDuration(120s) > block.timestamp (early unlisting condition met)
6. usdt.balanceOf(msg.sender) >= penaltyFee check: balanceOf(seller) >= 10*10^18 USDT units
7. With 6-decimal USDT, 10*10^18 = 10 trillion USDT. No user has this balance.
8. require REVERTS with 'SS_Marketplace: Penalty fee required for early unlisting'
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The path from constructor setting penaltyFee=10 ether to unlistVesting requiring that amount in 6-decimal USDT is direct and unconditional. The MarketplaceSetting constructor (line ~108) sets penaltyFee = 10 ether. unlistVesting reads this via IMarketplaceSetting(marketplaceSetting).penaltyFee() and requires the seller to hold and transfer that many USDT units. With 6-decimal USDT, this equals 10^13 USDT, which is impossible to satisfy.

deployment_assumptions_checked: Admin does not call setPenaltyFee after deployment
config_validation.deployment_evidence: DeploySecondSwap.s.sol creates MarketplaceSetting via `new SecondSwap_MarketplaceSetting(cfg.feeCollector, cfg.admin, ..., cfg.usdt)` but never calls setPenaltyFee afterward. The USDT address comes from SECOND_SWAP_USDT env var or a locally deployed TestToken1. For production deployments with real USDT (6 decimals on Ethereum/Base/Arbitrum), the default 10 ether penalty is mismatched. The Hardhat ignition scripts (DeployMarketplaceSetting.ts) also do not call setPenaltyFee.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: This is not an attack scenario but a configuration bug. After deployment with default settings and 6-decimal USDT: (1) Any seller lists vesting tokens. (2) Within 120 seconds (minListingDuration), seller wants to unlist. (3) unlistVesting checks USDT balance >= 10*10^18. (4) With 6-decimal USDT, this equals 10 trillion USDT. (5) Transaction reverts. (6) Seller's tokens are locked until minListingDuration passes. The DoS persists until admin calls setPenaltyFee with a value appropriate for 6-decimal USDT (e.g., 10 * 10^6 for $10).
validation_result.impact_assessment: All sellers who attempt early unlisting within minListingDuration (default 120 seconds) will have their transactions revert. Their vesting tokens remain locked in listings until the minimum duration passes or admin corrects the fee. While the minListingDuration is only 120 seconds (reducing impact), the fundamental code defect is real: the hardcoded default uses 18-decimal denomination for a 6-decimal token. Impact severity depends on whether admin notices and fixes before deployment to production, but the code itself is defective.
recommended_mitigations: Change the constructor to accept penaltyFee as a constructor parameter with explicit denomination, or set it to a value appropriate for 6-decimal tokens (e.g., 10 * 1e6 for $10 USDT)
Add a decimal-aware setter or validation that checks the USDT token's decimals() when setting the penalty fee
Add setPenaltyFee call to the deployment script immediately after MarketplaceSetting creation
Document the expected denomination of penaltyFee relative to the USDT token's decimals in the NatSpec
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy protocol using DeploySecondSwap.s.sol with SECOND_SWAP_USDT pointing to a 6-decimal USDT mock. 2. Create a vesting, list it via listVesting. 3. Immediately call unlistVesting within 120 seconds. 4. Observe revert on penalty fee check. 5. Verify penaltyFee() returns 10*10^18. 6. Calculate that 10^18/10^6 = 10^12 = 1 trillion USDT per unit, times 10 = 10 trillion USDT required.
uncertainties: TestToken1 (used when USDT env var is not set) might have 18 decimals, making the default correct for testing but wrong for production
The minListingDuration is only 120 seconds, so the window of DoS impact is relatively short - sellers only need to wait 2 minutes to unlist without penalty
Admin may have out-of-band processes to call setPenaltyFee immediately after deployment that are not captured in the scripts provided
additional_testing_needed: Verify TestToken1's decimals() return value to confirm whether test deployments are also affected
Test end-to-end unlistVesting with real 6-decimal USDT to confirm revert
Check if there are any other deployment scripts or post-deployment configuration procedures not provided
